$schema: Lead Record Schema — FEDSafe Retirement Lead Manager
$description: Schema for a single lead record. Fields are split into three groups: FOIA Source (public employee data from the USPS roster), Enrichment (data obtained from third-party providers like Apollo, ZoomInfo, etc.), and System (app-managed metadata).

lead._comment4: These fields are populated during the initial data import.
lead._comment5: 472,576 records from the US Postal Service employee roster.

id.description: Unique identifier for the lead. Auto-generated UUID on import. This is the PK used across all relationships (collections, campaigns, enrichment lookups).

first_name.description: Employee's legal first name as listed in the FOIA roster. All caps in source but we normalize to title case on import.

last_name.description: Employee's legal last name. Primary search field. Indexed for fast lookups.

middle_initial.description: Single character middle initial. Note: source column has a typo ('Intial'). ~30% of records have this as null.

occupation_title.description: Federal job title. Useful for filtering prospects by role — e.g., targeting 'POSTMASTER', 'SUPERVISOR', or 'MANAGER' titles for higher-value leads. All uppercase in source.

grade_level.description: Federal pay grade/step. Format is typically '{schedule}{grade}-{step}' (e.g., P9-05 = PS Schedule Grade 9 Step 5). Higher grades = higher seniority and salary. Key indicator for retirement readiness.

annual_salary.description: Annual base salary in USD (no cents). Critical filter for lead quality — higher salary = more retirement savings potential. $0 values may indicate part-time or recently onboarded employees.

hourly_rate.description: Hourly pay rate in USD. Derived from annual salary / 2080 hours. Useful for identifying part-time vs full-time employees.

facility_name.description: Name of the USPS facility where the employee works. Usually formatted as '{CITY} PO' (Post Office), '{CITY} DDP' (Distribution), or '{CITY} P&DC' (Processing & Distribution Center).

facility_address.description: Street address of the USPS facility. This is the WORK address, not the employee's home address. Useful for geographic targeting but not for direct mail to the individual.

facility_city.description: City of the USPS facility. Indexed for geographic filtering. All uppercase in source.

facility_state.description: Two-letter state/territory code. 54 unique values (50 states + DC + territories like PR, GU, VI, AS). Primary geographic filter for regional campaigns.

facility_zip_code.description: ZIP+4 code of the facility. Text type to preserve leading zeros and the +4 extension. Can be used for radius-based targeting.

entered_on_duty_date.description: Date the employee entered federal service. CRITICAL for retirement planning — employees with 20+ years of service are prime prospects. Calculate years_of_service = today - entered_on_duty_date.

source_file.description: Identifier for the import batch/file. Allows tracking which import populated this record, useful when re-importing updated rosters.

lead._comment_enrichment3: These fields are NULL after initial import and populated by

personal_email.description: Personal email address (NOT work/gov email). Federal employees have .usps.gov emails but those aren't useful for marketing. Enrichment providers match name + location to find personal email addresses.
personal_email.enrichment_notes: Apollo and ZoomInfo are the best sources for this. Match on: first_name + last_name + facility_state. Expect 30-50% match rate.

personal_phone.description: Personal phone number. Enrichment providers can sometimes return mobile vs landline distinction. Store in E.164 or formatted US format.
personal_phone.enrichment_notes: Phone numbers have lower match rates (~15-25%) than email. Lusha and Cognism tend to have better phone data. Some providers return multiple numbers — store the most confident one here.

personal_address.description: Employee's home/personal street address. Essential for direct mail campaigns. NOT the same as facility_address.
personal_address.enrichment_notes: People-search APIs (Whitepages, BeenVerified) are the best source. Match on full name + state. USPS National Change of Address (NCOA) can validate/update addresses.

personal_city.description: Home city. Often close to facility_city but not always — employees may commute from neighboring cities.

personal_state.description: Home state. Usually matches facility_state but not guaranteed for border-area employees.

personal_zip.description: Home ZIP code. Useful for direct mail and radius targeting from the employee's actual residence.

linkedin_url.description: LinkedIn profile URL. Many federal employees maintain LinkedIn profiles. Useful for verifying identity and for LinkedIn outreach campaigns.
linkedin_url.enrichment_notes: Apollo has the highest LinkedIn match rate. Most USPS workers below management level may not have LinkedIn profiles — expect ~10-20% match rate. Alternative: web scraper that Googles '{first_name} {last_name} {facility_city} site:linkedin.com' and extracts the profile URL from search results.

facebook_url.description: Facebook profile URL. Many USPS employees are active on Facebook. Useful for Facebook Ads custom audience targeting and direct messaging outreach.
facebook_url.enrichment_notes: Harder to find programmatically than LinkedIn due to Facebook's privacy settings. Best approach: scraper that searches Google for '{first_name} {last_name} {facility_city} site:facebook.com' and evaluates results. Expect ~5-15% match rate with confidence.

lat.description: Latitude of the lead's location. Can be geocoded from either facility_address (work) or personal_address (home) using Google Maps Geocoding API or Mapbox. Enables map-based visualization, radius search, and proximity-based campaign targeting.
lat.enrichment_notes: Geocode from facility address first (we always have it). Update to personal address lat/lon when enrichment provides home address. Google Geocoding API: $0.005/request. Mapbox: 100K free/month.

lon.description: Longitude of the lead's location. Paired with lat for geographic operations.

years_of_service.description: Years of federal service calculated as: (current_date - entered_on_duty_date) / 365.25. CRITICAL business metric — employees with 20-30+ years are closest to retirement and are the highest-value prospects. Can be computed on import and refreshed periodically.
years_of_service.enrichment_notes: This is a COMPUTED field, not from a third-party. Calculate during import: EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - entered_on_duty_date)) / 31557600. Update periodically or compute on-the-fly in queries.

age_estimate.description: Estimated age of the employee. Federal employees typically enter service between ages 18-35. Rough estimate: entered_on_duty_date + assumed_entry_age. More accurate estimates from people-search APIs.
age_estimate.enrichment_notes: Whitepages and BeenVerified sometimes return age ranges. For USPS, a reasonable estimate is: age ≈ (current_year - entered_on_duty_year) + 25 (average entry age). Employees aged 55-62 are in the retirement planning sweet spot.

do_not_contact.description: Opt-out / suppression flag. Set to TRUE if the lead has requested not to be contacted, or if they appear on a DNC (Do Not Call) list. Checked before any outreach. CRITICAL for CAN-SPAM and TCPA compliance.
do_not_contact.enrichment_notes: Cross-reference with the FTC National Do Not Call Registry and any internal suppression lists. Must be checked before phone or email outreach.

enrichment_source.description: Which provider was used to enrich this record. Important for tracking data quality and cost per enrichment.

enrichment_confidence.description: Confidence score from the enrichment provider (0.0 = no confidence, 1.0 = exact match). Records below 0.5 should be flagged for manual review.

enrichment_dt.description: Timestamp when this record was last enriched. Enrichment data gets stale — re-enrich records older than 6 months for active campaigns.

enrichment_status.description: Status of the enrichment attempt. 'partial' means some fields were found but not all. 'not_found' means the provider returned no match.

lead._comment_system3: Present on ALL tables. Auto-managed by triggers.

search_vector.description: PostgreSQL full-text search vector. Auto-populated by trigger on INSERT/UPDATE. Covers: first_name, last_name, middle_initial, occupation_title, facility_name, facility_city, facility_state, facility_zip_code. Indexed with GIN for fast text search.

cre_dt.description: Record creation timestamp. Set once on INSERT, never updated.

cre_by.description: User ID or identifier who created the record. 'data_import' for bulk imports, user email for manual entries.

mod_dt.description: Last modification timestamp. Auto-updated by trigger on every UPDATE.

mod_by.description: User ID or identifier who last modified the record.

version_no.description: Optimistic concurrency counter. Incremented by trigger on every UPDATE. Used to prevent lost updates when multiple users edit the same record.

tenant_id.description: Multi-tenancy partition key. NULL for now (single-tenant). Reserved for future SaaS expansion where multiple advisory firms share the platform.

_enrichment_strategy.overview: The FOIA data gives us name + work location + salary + tenure but NO personal contact info. To make these leads actionable for outreach, we need to enrich with personal email, phone, and home address.

recommended_providers.match_fields: first_name + last_name + company:'US Postal Service' + location:facility_state
recommended_providers.notes: Best overall value. Has a 'people search' API that can match by name + employer. USPS is a known employer in their database.

recommended_providers.match_fields: first_name + last_name + company:'United States Postal Service'
recommended_providers.notes: Highest match rates but very expensive. Worth it only if you plan to enrich 100K+ records regularly.
Best phone number accuracy. Smaller database than Apollo/ZoomInfo but higher phone match quality.
Good for finding work emails (@usps.gov) but less useful for personal emails. Use as a supplement.
Best source for home addresses. People-search data. Can return multiple matches — use facility_city proximity to pick the right one.
Good LinkedIn URL discovery. API supports bulk lookups.

recommended_approach: STEP 1: Start with Apollo.io (best free tier, good match rates). Enrich all 472K records using their bulk 'people search' API. Match on: first_name + last_name + company:'US Postal Service' + location:state.
STEP 2: For records Apollo couldn't match, try Whitepages Pro for home address + phone (their strength).
STEP 3: For high-value leads only (salary > $100K, years_of_service > 20), use Lusha or ZoomInfo for phone numbers.
STEP 4: Store enrichment_source, enrichment_confidence, and enrichment_dt so you know which provider found what and how fresh the data is.
STEP 5: Re-enrich records every 6 months for active campaigns — people move and change numbers.

cost_estimate.apollo_free_tier: 10K records/month × 0 cost = $0 (would take ~47 months for all records)
cost_estimate.tiered_approach: Enrich top 50K leads first (salary > $80K, 20+ years service) via Apollo free tier = 5 months at $0, then evaluate ROI before scaling
cost_estimate.recommendation: Start with tiered approach. Enrich the highest-value segment first and measure conversion before investing in full-database enrichment.
